description: Relying on the exit status of a pipeline, which by default reflects only the last command and masks an upstream failure.
body: Failure mode: in `producer | consumer`, the pipeline status is the consumer's, so if producer fails (e.g. curl errors but jq still exits 0) the script proceeds as if it succeeded. Detection signals: a failed download/command is treated as success; empty or partial data flows downstream; `set -e` does not stop the script. Do not assume a pipeline fails when an upstream stage does. Safer fallback: enable `set -o pipefail` (bash) so the pipeline returns the first non-zero status, check ${PIPESTATUS[@]} for per-stage status, or split the stages, then verify by forcing the producer to fail.
